intel_overview.headline_zh: UPC诉讼常态化，FRAND与专利无效成焦点
intel_overview.headline_en: UPC Litigation Normalizes; FRAND and Invalidity Take Center Stage
intel_overview.summary_zh: 2026年上半年欧洲IP战场呈现司法活跃与制度完善并行的态势。统一专利法院（UPC）案件量激增，尤其在SEP/FRAND费率裁定及专利无效宣告上展现高效执行力，同时PMAC调解机制上线丰富了争议解决路径。EPO通过引入DOCX提交、扩大成员国及优化审查质量提升服务效能。欧盟法院在商标声誉保护、在线版权报酬及反歧视领域确立新判例。整体而言，欧洲知识产权环境正趋向高标准合规与精细化维权，企业需应对更严格的专利稳定性审查及复杂的反垄断许可规则。
top_signals: UPC在SEP/FRAND纠纷中展现管辖权优势，但部分案件仍依赖成员国法院并行审理
EPO审查实践趋同完成第二轮，宽泛权利要求界定更严，授权质量KPI持续向好
瑞士专利法修订引入实质审查，欧专体系扩容至40国，摩尔多瓦即将加入
欧盟法院强化对商标恶意注册、在线内容公平报酬及基本权利的保护力度
cross_currents: 司法效率与法律确定性双重提升：UPC年报显示案件处理高效，配合EPO审查标准统一，降低了跨国专利布局的不确定性
合规成本与技术壁垒同步上升：从TTBER新规到瑞士实质审查，再到欧盟对AI和数字技术的专利倾斜，技术出海门槛提高
争议解决多元化趋势明显：UPC诉讼与PMAC调解仲裁并行，企业可根据案件性质选择更具性价比的维权或和解策略
implications: 针对UPC高频无效的医疗与通信专利，中国企业需前置进行FTO分析与专利稳定性评估，避免盲目维权导致高额费用
在SEP许可谈判中，应警惕历史低费率被认定为sub-FRAND的风险，利用新TTBER指南优化许可协议结构以符合反垄断合规
利用EPO DOCX提交便利及瑞士英文文档政策降低申请成本，同时关注摩尔多瓦等新成员国的市场准入机会
watchlist: CJEU关于前药SPC资格裁决的最终结果及其对药品专利延期的影响
UPC PMAC调解与仲裁服务在实际案件中的应用效果及案例积累
德国及英国法院在AI相关专利侵权及假冒商品执法中的最新判例动向

daily_report.headline_en: UPC Injunctions Tighten; TTBER New Rules Live
daily_report.executive_line: 本周UPC杜塞尔多夫分院提高临时禁令举证门槛，欧盟修订版TTBER正式生效明确SEP许可合规边界，瑞士专利法引入实质审查，执法与合规环境显著趋严。
daily_report.summary_zh: 过去48小时，欧洲IP战情聚焦司法实操与反垄断合规。UPC杜塞尔多夫分院裁定临时禁令需严格证明单一产品侵权，大幅抬高维权成本；同时，欧盟修订版TTBER生效，为专利池及FRAND许可提供清晰竞争法指引。此外，EPO重申商业规则非技术特征，德国警示AI恶意抢注风险，瑞士启动专利实质审查。企业需立即调整诉讼证据策略及许可协议结构以应对新规。

storylines.what_happened_zh: 修订版TTBER于5月1日生效，新增对专利池、数据许可及标准必要专利（SEP）的详细竞争法指导。
storylines.why_it_matters_zh: 为复杂许可协议提供“安全港”，企业需重新审查SEP协议以规避反垄断风险并优化集体谈判策略。

report_items.title_zh: EPO裁定：基于注册的启用属于非技术特征
report_items.title_en: EPO Board Rules Registration-Based Enablement Non-Technical in IoT Smoking System Case
report_items.title: Registration-based Enablement: Non-technical
report_items.summary_zh: 本案涉及菲利普莫里斯电热吸烟系统专利的异议程序。上诉委员会认定，设备需经互联网主机注册后方可启用的特征（特征1.7），仅反映非技术的商业或行政要求，不贡献于发明的技术特性。即便该要求导致设备运行状态发生物质变化，其底层概念的非技术性质不变。此裁决明确了在评估创造性时，纯粹由商业规则驱动的功能性限制将被排除在技术贡献之外。
report_items.summary_en: This case concerns patent proceedings for Philip Morris’ electrically heated smoking system. The Technical Board of Appeal determined that the feature requiring device enablement only after registration via an Internet host constitutes a non-technical business or administrative constraint. Consequently, this requirement does not contribute to the technical character of the invention. The Board clarified that even if implementing such a registration mandate causes a material change in the device’s operational state, the underlying concept remains non-technical. This decision establishes that fu...
core_points_zh: 注册后启用被视为非技术商业约束，不赋予发明技术特性
实施商业规则导致的设备状态改变不改变其非技术本质
在评估创造性时，纯商业或行政要求不被视为对技术的贡献
core_points_en: Registration-based enablement is classified as a non-technical business constraint lacking technical character.
Material changes in device state due to commercial rules do not alter the non-technical nature of the concept.
Purely commercial or administrative requirements are excluded from technical contributions in inventive step assessments.
report_items.insight_zh: 中国企业在欧申请IoT及智能硬件专利时，应避免将单纯的商业逻辑（如付费解锁、注册激活）作为核心技术特征。撰写时需深入挖掘实现这些功能背后的具体技术改进（如通信协议优化、安全加密算法），确保权利要求聚焦于解决技术问题，以防因缺乏技术贡献而在异议或无效程序中被驳回。
report_items.insight_en: For applicants in the IoT and smart hardware sectors, relying on pure commercial logic, such as paid unlocks or registration activation, as core technical features risks rejection for lack of technical contribution. Drafting strategies should instead focus on specific technical improvements enabling these functions, such as optimized communication protocols or security encryption algorithms. Claims must address concr...
insight_points_zh: 避免将纯商业逻辑作为核心技术创新点申报欧洲专利
聚焦实现功能的具体技术改进以确立专利的技术贡献
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid claiming pure commercial logic like registration as core innovation for European patents.
Practice note: Focus claims on specific technical implementations, such as protocol optimizations, to establish technical contribution.
What to monitor: Monitor EPO trends excluding administrative constraints from technical character assessments in IoT cases.

report_items.title_zh: UPC官方2025年度回顾：五大亮点解析
report_items.title_en: UPC 2025 Annual Review: Five Key Highlights for Patent Litigants and Rights Holders
report_items.title: Official UPC Year in Review – Five highlights from 2025
report_items.summary_zh: Bristows律所发布分析，解读统一专利法院（UPC）发布的2025年官方年度回顾报告。该报告是UPC的第二份年度报告，全面梳理了2025年的机构活动、案件趋势及运营发展。文章从中提炼出五个对UPC用户至关重要的亮点，旨在帮助利益相关者更好地理解UPC的最新动态与司法实践走向，为在欧专利诉讼策略提供参考依据。
report_items.summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) has published its second official Annual Review, offering a comprehensive overview of institutional activities, case trends, and operational developments throughout 2025. This report serves as a critical resource for understanding the court's evolving jurisprudence and procedural efficiency. Bristows analyzes the document to extract five significant highlights that are particularly relevant for UPC users. These insights provide legal practitioners and corporate counsel with essential data to navigate the complex landscape of European patent litigation. By examini...
core_points_zh: UPC发布第二份官方年度回顾，全面展示2025年机构活动与案件趋势
报告详细分析了UPC的运营发展状况，为法律从业者提供关键数据支持
文章提炼五大核心亮点，聚焦对UPC用户最具参考价值的实务动态
core_points_en: The UPC’s second annual review comprehensively details institutional activities and emerging case trends observed during 2025.
Operational developments and statistical data are analyzed to provide legal practitioners with crucial support for case management.
Five core highlights are extracted to focus on practical dynamics offering the most value to current and prospective UPC users.
report_items.insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
report_items.insight_en: Rights holders and market participants should closely monitor the case trends and procedural shifts outlined in the UPC’s annual review. Particular attention is warranted regarding judicial practices concerning preliminary injunctions and jurisdictional matters. It is advisable to proactively assess patent portfolio risks within Europe and optimize litigation response strategies. Leveraging these insights allows comp...
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor UPC case trends to optimize European patent litigation and compliance response strategies.
Practice note: Utilize annual review insights to anticipate judicial practice, proactively avoid infringement risks, and protect rights.
What to monitor: Developments in preliminary injunction standards and jurisdictional rulings affecting cross-border enforcement.

report_items.title_zh: 英国上诉法院就选择性分销体系在线限制发布指引
report_items.title_en: UK Court of Appeal Clarifies Legality of Online Restrictions in Selective Distribution
report_items.title: UK Court of Appeal issues guidance on online restrictions in selective distribution systems
report_items.summary_zh: 英国上诉法院在Deckers诉Up & Running案中裁定，品牌方禁止授权零售商在未品牌折扣网站销售不违反竞争法，推翻竞争上诉法庭原判。法院确认此类在线限制需结合法律与经济背景评估损害程度，而非自动违法。此举虽未放松对转售价格维持等核心限制的执法，但强调品牌应建立透明、客观的分销标准以规避诉讼风险。
report_items.summary_en: The UK Court of Appeal ruled in Deckers UK Limited v Up & Running (UK) Limited that preventing an authorised retailer from selling on unbranded discount websites does not automatically breach competition law. This decision overturns the Competition Appeal Tribunal’s earlier judgment, confirming that restrictions within selective distribution systems require a nuanced assessment of their legal and economic context to establish sufficient harm. The ruling aligns with established case law such as Coty, emphasizing that online sales limitations are not presumed illegal. However, this does not sign...
core_points_zh: 在线销售限制非自动违法，需个案评估法律经济背景及损害程度
上诉法院推翻初审判决，支持品牌方管控未品牌折扣渠道的合规性
企业应避免价格固定等核心限制，通过透明标准降低反垄断诉讼风险
core_points_en: Online sales restrictions in selective distribution are not automatically illegal but require contextual assessment.
The Court of Appeal overturned the CAT, supporting brand control over unbranded discount channels.
Companies should avoid hardcore restrictions like price fixing and adopt transparent distribution criteria.
report_items.insight_zh: 中国出口欧洲的品牌商需警惕选择性分销中的在线限制合规性。建议避免直接禁止线上销售或固定价格，转而制定基于品牌形象和防止搭便车的客观准入标准。完善合同条款与证据留存，可有效应对潜在的反垄断调查与民事诉讼，保障供应链稳定。
report_items.insight_en: Market participants operating selective distribution systems in Europe must carefully distinguish between permissible qualitative criteria and prohibited hardcore restrictions. While brands retain discretion to protect their image by restricting sales on unbranded discount platforms, they must document objective justifications linked to brand positioning or preventing free-riding. Blanket bans on online sales or any...
insight_points_zh: 制定基于品牌定位的客观分销标准，避免 blanket ban 线上销售
严禁转售价格维持等核心限制，完善合规文档以应对反垄断审查
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Brands must define objective, transparent criteria for online channel access to justify restrictions without infringing competition law.
Practice note: Avoid blanket online sales bans or price-fixing; instead, base restrictions on brand integrity and prevention of free-riding.
What to monitor: Enforcement trends regarding hardcore restrictions and judicial interpretations of 'sufficient harm' in digital markets.

report_items.title_zh: CJEU裁决请求：前药是否可独立获SPC保护？
report_items.title_en: CJEU Asked to Rule on Whether Pro-Drugs Qualify for Independent SPC Protection
report_items.title: Pro = Active? CJEU Asked To Decide
report_items.summary_zh: 爱尔兰律所HGF分析指出，CJEU已受理案件（C-794/25），需裁定已获批活性成分的前药能否独立获得补充保护证书（SPC）。背景涉及武田制药的LDX（右旋安非他命前药）与母药DEX。尽管LDX作为新活性物质获批上市，但法规对前药是否属“同一活性物质”存疑。此裁决将澄清行业争议，解决相关诉讼，明确前药在欧盟的SPC资格标准。
report_items.summary_en: The Court of Justice of the European Union has accepted a referral in case C-794/25, seeking clarification on whether an approved pro-drug of a previously authorized active pharmaceutical ingredient can independently obtain Supplementary Protection Certificate (SPC) protection. The dispute centers on lisdexamfetamine (LDX), a pro-drug marketed by Takeda, and its parent compound dexamphetamine (DEX). Although LDX received marketing authorization as a New Active Substance due to distinct safety and efficacy profiles, questions remain regarding its status under EU regulations. Specifically, it is...
core_points_zh: CJEU受理前药SPC资格案，需裁定前药能否独立于母药获保护
争议焦点在于前药是否因安全性或疗效显著不同而视为新活性物质
裁决结果将消除法律不确定性，直接影响欧盟药品专利策略布局
core_points_en: The CJEU must determine if a pro-drug qualifies for independent SPC protection separate from its parent active ingredient.
The core legal issue involves assessing whether safety or efficacy differences justify treating the pro-drug as a new active substance.
The final judgment will eliminate legal uncertainty and directly influence pharmaceutical patent strategy within the EU market.
report_items.insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
report_items.insight_en: Rights holders should closely monitor this precedent, as the outcome significantly impacts SPC eligibility for pro-drug pipelines in Europe. If pro-drugs are deemed ineligible for independent protection, companies may face reduced market exclusivity periods. Consequently, stakeholders should evaluate alternative strategies to extend commercial advantages, such as optimizing patent portfolios or leveraging other regul...
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assess SPC extension risks for pro-drug development pipelines operating within the European jurisdiction.
Practice note: Strengthen evidence chains demonstrating significant safety and efficacy differences to support new active substance classification.
What to monitor: Judicial interpretation of pro-drug status and its impact on future supplementary protection certificate applications.

report_items.title_zh: 德国商标申请中的恶意注册：企业日益增长的风险
report_items.title_en: Bad Faith Trademark Filings in Germany: Rising Risks and Strategic Implications
report_items.title: Bösgläubigkeit (bad faith) in trademark filings: A growing risk for businesses
report_items.summary_zh: 文章分析德国商标法中“恶意”（Bösgläubigkeit）概念的应用与风险。德国专利商标局指出，自2025年中以来，针对未注册商标的恶意申请激增，常利用AI工具识别目标并通过电商平台施压。依据《德国商标法》，缺乏真实使用意图且旨在阻碍竞争或获取不当利益的申请将被驳回或宣告无效。该认定需综合评估申请人主观意图与客观情况，受欧盟判例影响显著。
report_items.summary_en: This analysis examines the application of 'Bösgläubigkeit' (bad faith) within German trademark law. The German Patent and Trade Mark Office reports a surge in bad-faith applications since mid-2025, often targeting unregistered signs identified via AI tools to exert pressure through online platforms. Under the German Trademark Act, applications lacking genuine use intent and aimed at obstructing competition or securing undue advantages are subject to refusal or invalidation. Determining bad faith requires a comprehensive assessment combining subjective intent with objective circumstances. This...
core_points_zh: 德国专利商标局警示恶意商标申请激增，多利用AI识别未注册标志进行策略性阻击
德国商标法规定，无真实使用意图且旨在滥用权利的注册可被驳回或在事后宣告无效
恶意认定需结合主观意图与客观环境综合评估，德国司法实践深受欧盟法院判例影响
core_points_en: The DPMA warns of increased bad-faith filings using AI to target unregistered signs for strategic blocking purposes.
German law allows refusal or invalidation of registrations lacking genuine use intent and aiming to abuse rights.
Bad faith determination combines subjective intent with objective context, heavily influenced by EU judicial precedents.
report_items.insight_zh: 对在欧中国企业而言，应尽早完成核心品牌在德注册以确立权利基础，避免被抢注。同时需建立主动监控机制，警惕竞争对手利用恶意注册通过电商平台快速下架产品。面对此类指控，应积极收集在先使用证据，援引德国商标法关于恶意的规定提起无效宣告请求，维护市场地位。
report_items.insight_en: Market participants should secure early trademark registration in Germany to establish robust rights bases against potential squatting. Implementing active monitoring systems is crucial to detect potentially bad-faith filings that could lead to rapid product removals on e-commerce platforms. If facing such allegations or actions, rights holders must diligently collect evidence of prior use. Leveraging German statutor...
insight_points_zh: 尽早完成德国商标注册并建立监控机制，防范恶意抢注带来的电商下架风险
遭遇恶意指控时，应积极收集在先使用证据，依法提起无效宣告以维权
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Secure early German registration and monitor filings to prevent bad-faith squatting and e-commerce takedowns.
Practice note: Collect prior use evidence promptly to support invalidation actions against abusive trademark registrations.
What to monitor: Track DPMA notices and EU case law developments regarding bad faith definitions and enforcement trends.

report_items.title_zh: 如何规划成功的知识产权调解
report_items.title_en: Strategic IP Mediation: Leveraging EUIPO and UPC Mechanisms for Efficient Dispute Resolution
report_items.title: How to plan successful IP mediation
report_items.summary_zh: 文章指出调解因高效、保密及商业导向优势，成为解决IP纠纷的优选。WIPO数据显示2025年案件激增42%，中小企业占比高。欧盟层面，EUIPO扩大调解服务范围，UPC将于2026年设立专利调解中心。调解由中立专家主持，灵活处理跨法域冲突，提供法院无法给予的商业解决方案，有助于建立双赢局面。
report_items.summary_en: Mediation has emerged as a preferred method for resolving intellectual property disputes, offering efficiency, confidentiality, and commercially oriented solutions. Data from the World Intellectual Property Organization indicates a significant 42% increase in administered cases in 2025, with small and medium-sized enterprises representing a substantial portion of participants. In Europe, the European Union Intellectual Property Office has expanded its mediation services to cover all inter partes proceedings for EU trademarks and designs. Furthermore, the Unified Patent Court is set to inaugura...
core_points_zh: 调解具高效保密优势，WIPO案件量增42%，中小企参与度高
EUIPO扩大调解范围，UPC将于2026年设立专利调解仲裁中心
调解灵活处理跨法域冲突，提供超越诉讼的商业双赢方案
core_points_en: WIPO reported a 42% surge in IP mediation cases in 2025, with SMEs and individuals comprising the majority of involved parties.
The EUIPO expanded its mediation scope to all inter partes trademark and design proceedings, while the UPC will launch its patent mediation centre in 2026.
Mediation offers a flexible, confidential alternative to litigation, facilitating cross-border resolutions and creating commercial win-win opportunities.
report_items.insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临IP纠纷时，应优先考虑利用EUIPO及即将成立的UPC调解机制。相比传统诉讼，调解成本更低且能保护商业秘密，尤其适合中小企业。建议提前在合同中加入调解条款，并熟悉WIPO等机构的规则，以灵活应对侵权或许可谈判，避免高昂 litigation 风险。
report_items.insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe should strategically consider alternative dispute resolution mechanisms to manage IP risks effectively. The expansion of EUIPO mediation services and the upcoming UPC Patent Mediation Centre provide accessible, cost-effective forums for resolving conflicts without the adversarial nature of court proceedings. This approach is particularly advantageous for protecting trade secret...
insight_points_zh: 优先利用EUIPO及UPC调解机制降低在欧维权成本与风险
建议在合同中预设调解条款，灵活运用ADR解决跨境IP争议
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Utilizing EUIPO and upcoming UPC mediation mechanisms can significantly reduce enforcement costs and litigation risks for rights holders in Europe.
Practice note: Draft contracts with pre-agreed mediation clauses to ensure access to efficient, confidential dispute resolution processes when conflicts arise.
What to monitor: Track the operational rollout of the UPC Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre in 2026 and evolving procedural guidelines from major ADR providers.

report_items.title_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫分院裁定：临时禁令需证明单一产品侵权
report_items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf Dismisses Preliminary Injunction: Strict Single-Product Infringement Proof Required
report_items.title: LD Düsseldorf, May 12, 2026, Order, UPC_CFI_1747/2025
report_items.summary_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫地方法院在2026年5月12日裁决中驳回申请人临时禁令请求。法院强调，申请人须充分证明侵权行为发生的可能性大于不可能性。针对隐形牙套专利，法院认定所有技术特征必须体现在单个产品中，不同阶段的牙套不能组合认定侵权。因申请人仅依赖宣传材料且缺乏具体技术分析，未能满足举证标准，法院未审理有效性等其他抗辩理由。
report_items.summary_en: The UPC Local Division in Düsseldorf dismissed an applicant’s request for preliminary injunctions on May 12, 2026. The Court emphasized that applicants must demonstrate with sufficient certainty that infringement is more likely than not. Regarding clear aligner patents, the ruling established that all technical features of a method claim must be embodied within a single product. Consequently, different treatment phases executed by separate aligners cannot be combined to establish infringement. Since the applicant relied exclusively on promotional materials without providing specific technical...
core_points_zh: 申请人承担临时禁令侵权举证责任，需证明侵权可能性大于不可能性
方法权利要求的所有特征必须在单个对齐器中实现，不可跨阶段组合
仅凭宣传材料和推测性证言不足以确立复杂技术领域的侵权事实
core_points_en: Applicants bear the burden of proof in provisional measures, requiring demonstration that infringement is more likely than not.
All method claim features must be realized within a single aligner; features across different treatment stages cannot be aggregated.
Promotional materials and speculative witness statements are insufficient to prove infringement in complex technical fields.
report_items.insight_zh: 中国医疗器械企业出口欧洲时，应警惕UPC对‘单一产品’特征的严格解释。在应对临时禁令指控时，需准备详尽的技术比对分析，避免仅依赖营销材料。建议提前进行FTO分析，确保多阶段治疗方案不落入同一专利的单一产品权利要求范围，以规避高额诉讼风险。
report_items.insight_en: This decision underscores the UPC’s strict interpretation of 'single product' requirements for multi-stage medical treatments like clear aligner therapy. Parties seeking or defending against preliminary injunctions must prepare detailed, feature-by-feature technical comparisons rather than relying on marketing collateral. For companies operating in Europe, this highlights the necessity of conducting thorough Freedom-...
insight_points_zh: 重视UPC对单一产品特征比对的严格要求，避免跨阶段组合侵权风险
完善技术证据链，避免仅凭营销材料应对复杂的专利侵权指控
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Strict adherence to single-product infringement definitions is critical for multi-stage medical device strategies.
Practice note: Develop comprehensive technical evidence chains avoiding reliance on general marketing materials for legal defense.
What to monitor: UPC case law regarding the aggregation of features across distinct treatment phases in method claims.

report_items.title_zh: 瑞士通过专利法修订引入实质审查制度
report_items.title_en: Switzerland Reforms Patent Act: Mandatory Searches and Optional Substantive Examinations
report_items.title: Switzerland introduces substantive examinations with Patent Act reform
report_items.summary_zh: 2024年3月，瑞士联邦议会通过《专利法》部分修订案。此次改革核心在于引入强制性的现有技术检索，并允许申请人选择是否进行全面的实质审查。此外，新规接受英文技术文件，并建立了新的上诉系统。这一变革标志着瑞士从形式审查向更严格的审查模式转变，旨在提升专利质量与法律确定性，对在该国进行专利布局的企业将产生深远影响。
report_items.summary_en: In March 2024, the Swiss Federal Assembly passed a partial revision of the Patent Act, marking a significant shift in the national intellectual property framework. The reform introduces a mandatory prior-art search for all applications and establishes an optional full substantive examination procedure. Additionally, the updated law accepts technical documents in English and implements a new appeal system. These changes transition Switzerland from a purely formal examination model to a stricter review regime. The primary objectives are to enhance overall patent quality and increase legal certai...
core_points_zh: 瑞士联邦议会于2024年3月通过《专利法》部分修订案
引入强制性现有技术检索及可选的全面实质审查机制
接受英文技术文档并建立全新的专利上诉系统
core_points_en: The Swiss Federal Assembly approved a partial Patent Act revision in March 2024, introducing major procedural changes.
New rules mandate prior-art searches and offer applicants the option to request full substantive examination.
The reform accepts English-language technical documents and establishes a completely new patent appeal system.
report_items.insight_zh: 中国企业赴瑞士申请专利需适应新流程，强制检索可能增加前期成本与时间。建议提前规划现有技术分析，利用英文文件便利降低翻译成本。同时，关注实质审查的可选性，根据商业价值权衡投入，以应对更高的授权门槛和潜在的驳回风险。
report_items.insight_en: Market participants filing in Switzerland must adapt to the new mandatory prior-art search requirements, which may increase initial costs and processing times. Applicants should optimize early-stage prior-art analysis and budget planning to accommodate these changes. The acceptance of English technical documents offers an opportunity to reduce translation expenses, provided filings are managed efficiently. Furthermor...
insight_points_zh: 适应强制检索要求，优化前期现有技术分析与预算规划
善用英文文件政策降低成本，审慎评估实质审查的商业必要性
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Adapt to mandatory prior-art search requirements by optimizing early-stage analysis and budget planning.
Practice note: Leverage the acceptance of English technical documents to reduce translation costs while ensuring compliance.
What to monitor: Assess the commercial necessity of optional substantive examination against higher authorization thresholds.

report_items.title_zh: 修订版TTBER下技术许可：专利池、FRAND及集体谈判指南更清晰
report_items.title_en: Revised EU TTBER: Enhanced Guidance on Patent Pools, FRAND, and Collective Negotiations
report_items.title: Technology licensing under the revised TTBER: clearer guidance for pools, FRAND and collective negotiations
report_items.summary_zh: 欧盟委员会于2026年4月16日通过修订版《技术转移集体豁免条例》(TTBER)及配套指南，将于5月1日生效。新规保留原有安全港结构，但细化了针对专利池、标准必要技术(SEP)许可及集体谈判的合规指导。对于涉及复杂许可模式的企业，需重点评估协议结构以降低反垄断风险，确保符合TFEU第101(3)条要求。
report_items.summary_en: On 16 April 2026, the European Commission adopted the revised Technology Transfer Block Exemption Regulation (TTBER) and accompanying Guidelines, effective 1 May 2026. This new framework replaces the 2014 rules, maintaining the established safe harbour structure under Article 101(3) TFEU for agreements meeting market share thresholds and avoiding hardcore restrictions. Significantly, the revised Guidelines provide detailed competition law guidance for modern licensing structures, including technology pools, standard-essential technology (SEP) licensing, data licensing, and licensing negotiatio...
core_points_zh: 修订版TTBER及指南于2026年5月1日生效，取代2014年框架
新增对专利池、数据许可及标准必要技术许可的详细竞争法指导
企业需优化复杂许可协议结构以规避反垄断风险并享受安全港保护
core_points_en: The revised TTBER and Guidelines enter into force on 1 May 2026, replacing the previous 2014 regulatory framework.
New provisions offer specific competition law guidance for patent pools, data licensing, and standard-essential technology arrangements.
Companies must optimize complex licensing agreement structures to ensure antitrust compliance and qualify for safe harbour protection.
report_items.insight_zh: 中国科技企业应密切关注新规对SEP许可及专利池的合规要求，特别是在参与集体谈判时。建议重新审查现有许可协议，确保不包含核心限制条款，并利用新指南优化FRAND谈判策略，以降低在欧面临的反垄断调查与诉讼风险。
report_items.insight_en: Stakeholders involved in technology licensing should closely monitor the updated compliance requirements for SEP licensing and patent pool participation, particularly regarding collective negotiation mechanisms. It is advisable to review existing licensing agreements to ensure they do not contain hardcore restrictions that could jeopardize block exemption status. Furthermore, parties should utilize the new Guidelines...
insight_points_zh: 重新审查SEP许可协议以符合新规反垄断合规要求
利用新指南优化专利池参与及集体谈判策略
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Review SEP licensing agreements to align with new antitrust compliance requirements under the revised TTBER.
Practice note: Leverage updated guidelines to optimize participation in patent pools and structure collective negotiation strategies effectively.
What to monitor: Evolving enforcement priorities regarding hardcore restrictions and compliance in complex technology transfer arrangements.

report_items.title_zh: CJEU：忽视入住率的版权费率过高或构成滥用市场支配地位
report_items.title_en: CJEU Rules Ignoring Hotel Occupancy in Copyright Fees May Abuse Dominant Position
report_items.title: Copyright royalty rates excessive of economic value may be abusive of dominant position, rules CJEU against Czech CMO
report_items.summary_zh: 欧盟法院裁定捷克集体管理组织OSA向酒店收取固定版权费未考虑房间入住率，可能违反TFEU第102条构成滥用支配地位。法院强调版权许可费应与被许可人实际获得的经济价值成比例，若行政上可行，忽略入住率等实际使用情况的定价属不公平高价。该裁决要求版权收费机制必须反映真实经济价值，对欧盟内版权许可实践具有深远影响。
report_items.summary_en: The Court of Justice of the EU ruled that a Czech collective management organisation’s practice of charging flat-rate copyright fees without considering hotel room occupancy may constitute an abuse of dominant position under Article 102 TFEU. The Court emphasized that royalty rates must be proportionate to the actual economic value received by licensees and the specific use of protected works. Ignoring factual usage indicators, such as occupancy rates or seasonal closures, creates a disconnect between price and value. Where administratively feasible, pricing models must reflect these realities...
core_points_zh: CJEU裁定忽略入住率的固定版权费率可能构成滥用市场支配地位
版权许可费必须与被许可人实际获得的经济价值及作品使用情况成比例
若行政可行，集体管理组织需采用基于实际使用（如入住率）的计费方法
core_points_en: Flat-rate copyright fees ignoring hotel occupancy may abuse dominant position under EU competition law.
Royalty rates must be proportionate to the actual economic value and specific use of protected works.
Collective management organisations should adopt usage-based billing methods where administratively feasible.
report_items.insight_zh: 在欧运营酒店、餐饮及娱乐设施的中国企业应重新审查现有版权许可协议。若支付固定费用且未挂钩实际使用情况，可依据此判例主张费率不公并寻求调整。建议评估历史缴费记录，必要时通过谈判或法律途径争取更公平的计费方式，以降低合规成本并规避反垄断风险。
report_items.insight_en: Rights holders and licensees operating in the EU should review existing copyright licensing agreements, particularly those involving fixed fees disconnected from actual usage metrics like occupancy. This CJEU precedent provides a basis to challenge unfair pricing structures that fail to reflect the real economic value derived from protected works. Stakeholders may leverage this ruling during negotiations to secure mo...
insight_points_zh: 在欧中企应审查版权许可费是否与实际使用情况挂钩以规避反垄断风险
可依据CJEU判例主张固定费率不公，通过谈判或诉讼争取合理计费方式
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Licensees can challenge fixed fees lacking usage links to mitigate antitrust risks.
Practice note: Use CJEU precedent to negotiate fairer, usage-based copyright royalty adjustments.
What to monitor: Enforcement trends regarding disproportionate royalties by collective management organisations.

weekly_report.headline_en: UPC Injunctions Tighten; TTBER New Rules Live
weekly_report.executive_line: 本周UPC提高临时禁令举证门槛，欧盟修订技术许可反垄断指南，瑞士引入专利实质审查，合规与诉讼风险显著上升。
weekly_report.summary_zh: 本周欧洲IP环境呈现‘司法趋严、规则细化’态势。UPC杜塞尔多夫分院明确临时禁令需证明单一产品侵权，大幅提高举证标准；欧盟新版TTBER生效，为SEP及专利池提供清晰反垄断指引。同时，瑞士启动专利实质审查改革，德国警示AI恶意抢注风险。企业需立即调整在欧许可策略与证据链管理，以应对更高的合规与诉讼挑战。

storylines.title_zh: 欧盟修订技术许可反垄断指南
storylines.what_happened_zh: 修订版TTBER于5月1日生效，新增对专利池、数据许可及FRAND谈判的详细竞争法指导与安全港规则。
storylines.why_it_matters_zh: 为复杂许可协议提供明确合规路径，企业需重新审查SEP许可结构以规避反垄断调查风险。

supporting_items.title_zh: 修订版TTBER下技术许可：专利池、FRAND及集体谈判指南更清晰
supporting_items.title: Technology licensing under the revised TTBER: clearer guidance for pools, FRAND and collective negotiations

storylines.title_en: EU Revises Tech Licensing Antitrust Guidelines
storylines.what_happened_en: The revised TTBER and Guidelines took effect on May 1, introducing specific competition law guidance and safe harbors for patent pools, data licensing, and FRAND negotiations.
storylines.why_it_matters_en: Companies must restructure SEP licensing agreements to align with new antitrust requirements, ensuring compliance and avoiding regulatory investigations.

storylines.title_zh: UPC提高临时禁令举证门槛
storylines.what_happened_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫分院裁定，申请人须证明单一产品中实现所有方法特征，禁止跨阶段组合推断侵权。
storylines.why_it_matters_zh: 显著增加权利人获取快速禁令的难度，迫使企业完善具体技术比对证据，避免依赖营销材料。

supporting_items.title_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫分院裁定：临时禁令需证明单一产品侵权
supporting_items.title: LD Düsseldorf, May 12, 2026, Order, UPC_CFI_1747/2025

storylines.title_en: UPC Raises Preliminary Injunction Evidentiary Bar
storylines.what_happened_en: The UPC Düsseldorf division ruled that applicants must prove all method claim features exist within a single product, prohibiting aggregation across different stages.
storylines.why_it_matters_en: Securing fast injunctions is harder; rights holders must build robust technical evidence chains rather than relying on marketing materials or speculative assertions.

storylines.what_happened_zh: EPO裁定注册启用属非技术特征；CJEU受理前药SPC资格案并裁定忽视入住率的版权费率可能构成滥用。
storylines.why_it_matters_zh: 持续收紧纯商业逻辑的专利保护，强调知识产权价值需与实际经济贡献或技术改进挂钩。

storylines.title_en: EPO and CJEU Reinforce Technical Contribution Standards
storylines.what_happened_en: The EPO classified registration-based enablement as non-technical, while the CJEU accepted a pro-drug SPC referral and ruled against flat copyright fees ignoring usage.
storylines.why_it_matters_en: Purely commercial logic faces tighter patent scrutiny, emphasizing that IP value must link to actual technical improvements or proportional economic contributions.

storylines.title_zh: 瑞士引入专利实质审查制度
storylines.what_happened_zh: 瑞士通过《专利法》修订案，引入强制性现有技术检索及可选的全面实质审查机制，接受英文申请。
storylines.why_it_matters_zh: 改变瑞士专利授权生态，企业需优化前期检索预算，利用英文政策降低成本并审慎评估审查必要性。

supporting_items.title_zh: 瑞士通过专利法修订引入实质审查制度
supporting_items.title: Switzerland introduces substantive examinations with Patent Act reform

storylines.what_happened_en: Switzerland amended its Patent Act to mandate prior-art searches and allow optional full substantive examinations, accepting English technical documents.
storylines.why_it_matters_en: Applicants should optimize early search budgets and leverage English filings to reduce costs while assessing the necessity of substantive review.

storylines.title_en: UK Court Clarifies Online Selective Distribution Limits
storylines.what_happened_en: The UK Court of Appeal ruled that banning sales on unbranded discount sites does not automatically breach competition law, overturning the Competition Appeal Tribunal.
storylines.why_it_matters_en: Brands must define objective, transparent criteria for online access based on brand integrity, avoiding blanket bans or price-fixing to ensure compliance.

report_items.title_zh: EPO裁定：基于注册的启用属于非技术特征
report_items.title_en: EPO Board Rules Registration-Based Enablement Non-Technical in IoT Smoking System Case
report_items.title: Registration-based Enablement: Non-technical
report_items.summary_zh: 本案涉及菲利普莫里斯电热吸烟系统专利的异议程序。上诉委员会认定，设备需经互联网主机注册后方可启用的特征（特征1.7），仅反映非技术的商业或行政要求，不贡献于发明的技术特性。即便该要求导致设备运行状态发生物质变化，其底层概念的非技术性质不变。此裁决明确了在评估创造性时，纯粹由商业规则驱动的功能性限制将被排除在技术贡献之外。
report_items.summary_en: This case concerns patent proceedings for Philip Morris’ electrically heated smoking system. The Technical Board of Appeal determined that the feature requiring device enablement only after registration via an Internet host constitutes a non-technical business or administrative constraint. Consequently, this requirement does not contribute to the technical character of the invention. The Board clarified that even if implementing such a registration mandate causes a material change in the device’s operational state, the underlying concept remains non-technical. This decision establishes that fu...
core_points_zh: 注册后启用被视为非技术商业约束，不赋予发明技术特性
实施商业规则导致的设备状态改变不改变其非技术本质
在评估创造性时，纯商业或行政要求不被视为对技术的贡献
core_points_en: Registration-based enablement is classified as a non-technical business constraint lacking technical character.
Material changes in device state due to commercial rules do not alter the non-technical nature of the concept.
Purely commercial or administrative requirements are excluded from technical contributions in inventive step assessments.
report_items.insight_zh: 中国企业在欧申请IoT及智能硬件专利时，应避免将单纯的商业逻辑（如付费解锁、注册激活）作为核心技术特征。撰写时需深入挖掘实现这些功能背后的具体技术改进（如通信协议优化、安全加密算法），确保权利要求聚焦于解决技术问题，以防因缺乏技术贡献而在异议或无效程序中被驳回。
report_items.insight_en: For applicants in the IoT and smart hardware sectors, relying on pure commercial logic, such as paid unlocks or registration activation, as core technical features risks rejection for lack of technical contribution. Drafting strategies should instead focus on specific technical improvements enabling these functions, such as optimized communication protocols or security encryption algorithms. Claims must address concr...
insight_points_zh: 避免将纯商业逻辑作为核心技术创新点申报欧洲专利
聚焦实现功能的具体技术改进以确立专利的技术贡献
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid claiming pure commercial logic like registration as core innovation for European patents.
Practice note: Focus claims on specific technical implementations, such as protocol optimizations, to establish technical contribution.
What to monitor: Monitor EPO trends excluding administrative constraints from technical character assessments in IoT cases.

report_items.title_zh: UPC官方2025年度回顾：五大亮点解析
report_items.title_en: UPC 2025 Annual Review: Five Key Highlights for Patent Litigants and Rights Holders
report_items.title: Official UPC Year in Review – Five highlights from 2025
report_items.summary_zh: Bristows律所发布分析，解读统一专利法院（UPC）发布的2025年官方年度回顾报告。该报告是UPC的第二份年度报告，全面梳理了2025年的机构活动、案件趋势及运营发展。文章从中提炼出五个对UPC用户至关重要的亮点，旨在帮助利益相关者更好地理解UPC的最新动态与司法实践走向，为在欧专利诉讼策略提供参考依据。
report_items.summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) has published its second official Annual Review, offering a comprehensive overview of institutional activities, case trends, and operational developments throughout 2025. This report serves as a critical resource for understanding the court's evolving jurisprudence and procedural efficiency. Bristows analyzes the document to extract five significant highlights that are particularly relevant for UPC users. These insights provide legal practitioners and corporate counsel with essential data to navigate the complex landscape of European patent litigation. By examini...
core_points_zh: UPC发布第二份官方年度回顾，全面展示2025年机构活动与案件趋势
报告详细分析了UPC的运营发展状况，为法律从业者提供关键数据支持
文章提炼五大核心亮点，聚焦对UPC用户最具参考价值的实务动态
core_points_en: The UPC’s second annual review comprehensively details institutional activities and emerging case trends observed during 2025.
Operational developments and statistical data are analyzed to provide legal practitioners with crucial support for case management.
Five core highlights are extracted to focus on practical dynamics offering the most value to current and prospective UPC users.
report_items.insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
report_items.insight_en: Rights holders and market participants should closely monitor the case trends and procedural shifts outlined in the UPC’s annual review. Particular attention is warranted regarding judicial practices concerning preliminary injunctions and jurisdictional matters. It is advisable to proactively assess patent portfolio risks within Europe and optimize litigation response strategies. Leveraging these insights allows comp...
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Monitor UPC case trends to optimize European patent litigation and compliance response strategies.
Practice note: Utilize annual review insights to anticipate judicial practice, proactively avoid infringement risks, and protect rights.
What to monitor: Developments in preliminary injunction standards and jurisdictional rulings affecting cross-border enforcement.

report_items.title_zh: 英国上诉法院就选择性分销体系在线限制发布指引
report_items.title_en: UK Court of Appeal Clarifies Legality of Online Restrictions in Selective Distribution
report_items.title: UK Court of Appeal issues guidance on online restrictions in selective distribution systems
report_items.summary_zh: 英国上诉法院在Deckers诉Up & Running案中裁定，品牌方禁止授权零售商在未品牌折扣网站销售不违反竞争法，推翻竞争上诉法庭原判。法院确认此类在线限制需结合法律与经济背景评估损害程度，而非自动违法。此举虽未放松对转售价格维持等核心限制的执法，但强调品牌应建立透明、客观的分销标准以规避诉讼风险。
report_items.summary_en: The UK Court of Appeal ruled in Deckers UK Limited v Up & Running (UK) Limited that preventing an authorised retailer from selling on unbranded discount websites does not automatically breach competition law. This decision overturns the Competition Appeal Tribunal’s earlier judgment, confirming that restrictions within selective distribution systems require a nuanced assessment of their legal and economic context to establish sufficient harm. The ruling aligns with established case law such as Coty, emphasizing that online sales limitations are not presumed illegal. However, this does not sign...
core_points_zh: 在线销售限制非自动违法，需个案评估法律经济背景及损害程度
上诉法院推翻初审判决，支持品牌方管控未品牌折扣渠道的合规性
企业应避免价格固定等核心限制，通过透明标准降低反垄断诉讼风险
core_points_en: Online sales restrictions in selective distribution are not automatically illegal but require contextual assessment.
The Court of Appeal overturned the CAT, supporting brand control over unbranded discount channels.
Companies should avoid hardcore restrictions like price fixing and adopt transparent distribution criteria.
report_items.insight_zh: 中国出口欧洲的品牌商需警惕选择性分销中的在线限制合规性。建议避免直接禁止线上销售或固定价格，转而制定基于品牌形象和防止搭便车的客观准入标准。完善合同条款与证据留存，可有效应对潜在的反垄断调查与民事诉讼，保障供应链稳定。
report_items.insight_en: Market participants operating selective distribution systems in Europe must carefully distinguish between permissible qualitative criteria and prohibited hardcore restrictions. While brands retain discretion to protect their image by restricting sales on unbranded discount platforms, they must document objective justifications linked to brand positioning or preventing free-riding. Blanket bans on online sales or any...
insight_points_zh: 制定基于品牌定位的客观分销标准，避免 blanket ban 线上销售
严禁转售价格维持等核心限制，完善合规文档以应对反垄断审查
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Brands must define objective, transparent criteria for online channel access to justify restrictions without infringing competition law.
Practice note: Avoid blanket online sales bans or price-fixing; instead, base restrictions on brand integrity and prevention of free-riding.
What to monitor: Enforcement trends regarding hardcore restrictions and judicial interpretations of 'sufficient harm' in digital markets.

report_items.title_zh: CJEU裁决请求：前药是否可独立获SPC保护？
report_items.title_en: CJEU Asked to Rule on Whether Pro-Drugs Qualify for Independent SPC Protection
report_items.title: Pro = Active? CJEU Asked To Decide
report_items.summary_zh: 爱尔兰律所HGF分析指出，CJEU已受理案件（C-794/25），需裁定已获批活性成分的前药能否独立获得补充保护证书（SPC）。背景涉及武田制药的LDX（右旋安非他命前药）与母药DEX。尽管LDX作为新活性物质获批上市，但法规对前药是否属“同一活性物质”存疑。此裁决将澄清行业争议，解决相关诉讼，明确前药在欧盟的SPC资格标准。
report_items.summary_en: The Court of Justice of the European Union has accepted a referral in case C-794/25, seeking clarification on whether an approved pro-drug of a previously authorized active pharmaceutical ingredient can independently obtain Supplementary Protection Certificate (SPC) protection. The dispute centers on lisdexamfetamine (LDX), a pro-drug marketed by Takeda, and its parent compound dexamphetamine (DEX). Although LDX received marketing authorization as a New Active Substance due to distinct safety and efficacy profiles, questions remain regarding its status under EU regulations. Specifically, it is...
core_points_zh: CJEU受理前药SPC资格案，需裁定前药能否独立于母药获保护
争议焦点在于前药是否因安全性或疗效显著不同而视为新活性物质
裁决结果将消除法律不确定性，直接影响欧盟药品专利策略布局
core_points_en: The CJEU must determine if a pro-drug qualifies for independent SPC protection separate from its parent active ingredient.
The core legal issue involves assessing whether safety or efficacy differences justify treating the pro-drug as a new active substance.
The final judgment will eliminate legal uncertainty and directly influence pharmaceutical patent strategy within the EU market.
report_items.insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
report_items.insight_en: Rights holders should closely monitor this precedent, as the outcome significantly impacts SPC eligibility for pro-drug pipelines in Europe. If pro-drugs are deemed ineligible for independent protection, companies may face reduced market exclusivity periods. Consequently, stakeholders should evaluate alternative strategies to extend commercial advantages, such as optimizing patent portfolios or leveraging other regul...
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assess SPC extension risks for pro-drug development pipelines operating within the European jurisdiction.
Practice note: Strengthen evidence chains demonstrating significant safety and efficacy differences to support new active substance classification.
What to monitor: Judicial interpretation of pro-drug status and its impact on future supplementary protection certificate applications.

report_items.title_zh: 德国商标申请中的恶意注册：企业日益增长的风险
report_items.title_en: Bad Faith Trademark Filings in Germany: Rising Risks and Strategic Implications
report_items.title: Bösgläubigkeit (bad faith) in trademark filings: A growing risk for businesses
report_items.summary_zh: 文章分析德国商标法中“恶意”（Bösgläubigkeit）概念的应用与风险。德国专利商标局指出，自2025年中以来，针对未注册商标的恶意申请激增，常利用AI工具识别目标并通过电商平台施压。依据《德国商标法》，缺乏真实使用意图且旨在阻碍竞争或获取不当利益的申请将被驳回或宣告无效。该认定需综合评估申请人主观意图与客观情况，受欧盟判例影响显著。
report_items.summary_en: This analysis examines the application of 'Bösgläubigkeit' (bad faith) within German trademark law. The German Patent and Trade Mark Office reports a surge in bad-faith applications since mid-2025, often targeting unregistered signs identified via AI tools to exert pressure through online platforms. Under the German Trademark Act, applications lacking genuine use intent and aimed at obstructing competition or securing undue advantages are subject to refusal or invalidation. Determining bad faith requires a comprehensive assessment combining subjective intent with objective circumstances. This...
core_points_zh: 德国专利商标局警示恶意商标申请激增，多利用AI识别未注册标志进行策略性阻击
德国商标法规定，无真实使用意图且旨在滥用权利的注册可被驳回或在事后宣告无效
恶意认定需结合主观意图与客观环境综合评估，德国司法实践深受欧盟法院判例影响
core_points_en: The DPMA warns of increased bad-faith filings using AI to target unregistered signs for strategic blocking purposes.
German law allows refusal or invalidation of registrations lacking genuine use intent and aiming to abuse rights.
Bad faith determination combines subjective intent with objective context, heavily influenced by EU judicial precedents.
report_items.insight_zh: 对在欧中国企业而言，应尽早完成核心品牌在德注册以确立权利基础，避免被抢注。同时需建立主动监控机制，警惕竞争对手利用恶意注册通过电商平台快速下架产品。面对此类指控，应积极收集在先使用证据，援引德国商标法关于恶意的规定提起无效宣告请求，维护市场地位。
report_items.insight_en: Market participants should secure early trademark registration in Germany to establish robust rights bases against potential squatting. Implementing active monitoring systems is crucial to detect potentially bad-faith filings that could lead to rapid product removals on e-commerce platforms. If facing such allegations or actions, rights holders must diligently collect evidence of prior use. Leveraging German statutor...

report_items.summary_en: Mediation has emerged as a preferred method for resolving intellectual property disputes, offering efficiency, confidentiality, and commercially oriented solutions. Data from the World Intellectual Property Organization indicates a significant 42% increase in administered cases in 2025, with small and medium-sized enterprises representing a substantial portion of participants. In Europe, the European Union Intellectual Property Office has expanded its mediation services to cover all inter partes proceedings for EU trademarks and designs. Furthermore, the Unified Patent Court is set to inaugura...

EUIPO扩大调解范围，UPC将于2026年设立专利调解仲裁中心
调解灵活处理跨法域冲突，提供超越诉讼的商业双赢方案

The EUIPO expanded its mediation scope to all inter partes trademark and design proceedings, while the UPC will launch its patent mediation centre in 2026.
Mediation offers a flexible, confidential alternative to litigation, facilitating cross-border resolutions and creating commercial win-win opportunities.

report_items.insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe should strategically consider alternative dispute resolution mechanisms to manage IP risks effectively. The expansion of EUIPO mediation services and the upcoming UPC Patent Mediation Centre provide accessible, cost-effective forums for resolving conflicts without the adversarial nature of court proceedings. This approach is particularly advantageous for protecting trade secret...
insight_points_zh: 优先利用EUIPO及UPC调解机制降低在欧维权成本与风险

insight_points_en: Client relevance: Utilizing EUIPO and upcoming UPC mediation mechanisms can significantly reduce enforcement costs and litigation risks for rights holders in Europe.
Practice note: Draft contracts with pre-agreed mediation clauses to ensure access to efficient, confidential dispute resolution processes when conflicts arise.
What to monitor: Track the operational rollout of the UPC Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre in 2026 and evolving procedural guidelines from major ADR providers.

report_items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf Dismisses Preliminary Injunction: Strict Single-Product Infringement Proof Required
report_items.title: LD Düsseldorf, May 12, 2026, Order, UPC_CFI_1747/2025
report_items.summary_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫地方法院在2026年5月12日裁决中驳回申请人临时禁令请求。法院强调，申请人须充分证明侵权行为发生的可能性大于不可能性。针对隐形牙套专利，法院认定所有技术特征必须体现在单个产品中，不同阶段的牙套不能组合认定侵权。因申请人仅依赖宣传材料且缺乏具体技术分析，未能满足举证标准，法院未审理有效性等其他抗辩理由。
report_items.summary_en: The UPC Local Division in Düsseldorf dismissed an applicant’s request for preliminary injunctions on May 12, 2026. The Court emphasized that applicants must demonstrate with sufficient certainty that infringement is more likely than not. Regarding clear aligner patents, the ruling established that all technical features of a method claim must be embodied within a single product. Consequently, different treatment phases executed by separate aligners cannot be combined to establish infringement. Since the applicant relied exclusively on promotional materials without providing specific technical...
core_points_zh: 申请人承担临时禁令侵权举证责任，需证明侵权可能性大于不可能性
方法权利要求的所有特征必须在单个对齐器中实现，不可跨阶段组合
仅凭宣传材料和推测性证言不足以确立复杂技术领域的侵权事实
core_points_en: Applicants bear the burden of proof in provisional measures, requiring demonstration that infringement is more likely than not.
All method claim features must be realized within a single aligner; features across different treatment stages cannot be aggregated.
Promotional materials and speculative witness statements are insufficient to prove infringement in complex technical fields.
report_items.insight_zh: 中国医疗器械企业出口欧洲时，应警惕UPC对‘单一产品’特征的严格解释。在应对临时禁令指控时，需准备详尽的技术比对分析，避免仅依赖营销材料。建议提前进行FTO分析，确保多阶段治疗方案不落入同一专利的单一产品权利要求范围，以规避高额诉讼风险。
report_items.insight_en: This decision underscores the UPC’s strict interpretation of 'single product' requirements for multi-stage medical treatments like clear aligner therapy. Parties seeking or defending against preliminary injunctions must prepare detailed, feature-by-feature technical comparisons rather than relying on marketing collateral. For companies operating in Europe, this highlights the necessity of conducting thorough Freedom-...
insight_points_zh: 重视UPC对单一产品特征比对的严格要求，避免跨阶段组合侵权风险
完善技术证据链，避免仅凭营销材料应对复杂的专利侵权指控
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Strict adherence to single-product infringement definitions is critical for multi-stage medical device strategies.
Practice note: Develop comprehensive technical evidence chains avoiding reliance on general marketing materials for legal defense.
What to monitor: UPC case law regarding the aggregation of features across distinct treatment phases in method claims.

report_items.summary_zh: 2024年3月，瑞士联邦议会通过《专利法》部分修订案。此次改革核心在于引入强制性的现有技术检索，并允许申请人选择是否进行全面的实质审查。此外，新规接受英文技术文件，并建立了新的上诉系统。这一变革标志着瑞士从形式审查向更严格的审查模式转变，旨在提升专利质量与法律确定性，对在该国进行专利布局的企业将产生深远影响。
report_items.summary_en: In March 2024, the Swiss Federal Assembly passed a partial revision of the Patent Act, marking a significant shift in the national intellectual property framework. The reform introduces a mandatory prior-art search for all applications and establishes an optional full substantive examination procedure. Additionally, the updated law accepts technical documents in English and implements a new appeal system. These changes transition Switzerland from a purely formal examination model to a stricter review regime. The primary objectives are to enhance overall patent quality and increase legal certai...

引入强制性现有技术检索及可选的全面实质审查机制
接受英文技术文档并建立全新的专利上诉系统
core_points_en: The Swiss Federal Assembly approved a partial Patent Act revision in March 2024, introducing major procedural changes.
New rules mandate prior-art searches and offer applicants the option to request full substantive examination.
The reform accepts English-language technical documents and establishes a completely new patent appeal system.
report_items.insight_zh: 中国企业赴瑞士申请专利需适应新流程，强制检索可能增加前期成本与时间。建议提前规划现有技术分析，利用英文文件便利降低翻译成本。同时，关注实质审查的可选性，根据商业价值权衡投入，以应对更高的授权门槛和潜在的驳回风险。
report_items.insight_en: Market participants filing in Switzerland must adapt to the new mandatory prior-art search requirements, which may increase initial costs and processing times. Applicants should optimize early-stage prior-art analysis and budget planning to accommodate these changes. The acceptance of English technical documents offers an opportunity to reduce translation expenses, provided filings are managed efficiently. Furthermor...
insight_points_zh: 适应强制检索要求，优化前期现有技术分析与预算规划
善用英文文件政策降低成本，审慎评估实质审查的商业必要性
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Adapt to mandatory prior-art search requirements by optimizing early-stage analysis and budget planning.
Practice note: Leverage the acceptance of English technical documents to reduce translation costs while ensuring compliance.
What to monitor: Assess the commercial necessity of optional substantive examination against higher authorization thresholds.

report_items.summary_en: On 16 April 2026, the European Commission adopted the revised Technology Transfer Block Exemption Regulation (TTBER) and accompanying Guidelines, effective 1 May 2026. This new framework replaces the 2014 rules, maintaining the established safe harbour structure under Article 101(3) TFEU for agreements meeting market share thresholds and avoiding hardcore restrictions. Significantly, the revised Guidelines provide detailed competition law guidance for modern licensing structures, including technology pools, standard-essential technology (SEP) licensing, data licensing, and licensing negotiatio...
core_points_zh: 修订版TTBER及指南于2026年5月1日生效，取代2014年框架
新增对专利池、数据许可及标准必要技术许可的详细竞争法指导
企业需优化复杂许可协议结构以规避反垄断风险并享受安全港保护
core_points_en: The revised TTBER and Guidelines enter into force on 1 May 2026, replacing the previous 2014 regulatory framework.
New provisions offer specific competition law guidance for patent pools, data licensing, and standard-essential technology arrangements.
Companies must optimize complex licensing agreement structures to ensure antitrust compliance and qualify for safe harbour protection.
report_items.insight_zh: 中国科技企业应密切关注新规对SEP许可及专利池的合规要求，特别是在参与集体谈判时。建议重新审查现有许可协议，确保不包含核心限制条款，并利用新指南优化FRAND谈判策略，以降低在欧面临的反垄断调查与诉讼风险。
report_items.insight_en: Stakeholders involved in technology licensing should closely monitor the updated compliance requirements for SEP licensing and patent pool participation, particularly regarding collective negotiation mechanisms. It is advisable to review existing licensing agreements to ensure they do not contain hardcore restrictions that could jeopardize block exemption status. Furthermore, parties should utilize the new Guidelines...

利用新指南优化专利池参与及集体谈判策略

What to monitor: Evolving enforcement priorities regarding hardcore restrictions and compliance in complex technology transfer arrangements.

transcript_lines_zh: 今天是2026年5月21日。以下是今天欧洲 IP 圈最重要的几条变化。
先看第一条，注册后启用被视为非技术商业约束，不赋予发明技术特性。值得关注的是，避免将纯商业逻辑作为核心技术创新点申报欧洲专利。
再看第二条，UPC发布第二份官方年度回顾，全面展示2025年机构活动与案件趋势。后续要看欧洲层面会不会出现进一步官方动作或配套安排。
再看第三条，在线销售限制非自动违法，需个案评估法律经济背景及损害程度。值得关注的是，制定基于品牌定位的客观分销标准，避免 blanket ban 线上销售。
最后看第四条，CJEU受理前药SPC资格案，需裁定前药能否独立于母药获保护。后续要看欧洲层面会不会出现进一步官方动作或配套安排。
总结来看，今天的欧洲 IP 动态仍然围绕专利、商标、SEP标准必要专利展开，接下来值得继续关注CJEU前药SPC案裁决时间表。

topics.headline_en: UPC 2026: PMAC Launch, Strict Equivalence Standards, and Procedural Compliance
topics.summary_zh: 2026年上半年，UPC发布年度报告并正式启用PMAC调解系统，强化ADR机制。司法实践趋严，明确等同侵权、单一产品比对及FRAND抗辩标准，程序合规要求提高。企业需警惕功能性限定风险，优化应诉策略，利用透明机制管理在欧专利布局与维权成本。
key_points: PMAC程序规则获批且案件管理系统上线，提供高效保密的调解仲裁服务，覆盖SEP/FRAND纠纷，降低维权成本。
司法判例细化等同原则与权利要求解释，强调单一产品特征比对，单独销售组件可能构成直接侵权，扩大保护边界。
程序合规审查趋严，明确上诉期限起算、撤诉规则及费用担保标准，严守时限与证据提交规范成为胜诉关键。
actions: 优先利用PMAC及EUIPO调解机制解决跨境争议，并在合同中预设调解条款以控制诉讼风险。
严格排查对欧出口产品的专利权利要求，避免使用易引发直接侵权的功能性限定语言。
建立严格的UPC诉讼期限双重核查机制，收到侵权通知立即书面表达许可意愿以保留FRAND抗辩权。
watchlist: 关注UPC主要分部早期判例固化趋势，预判未来专利法解释走向，避免单一法庭依赖。
监测欧洲层面围绕PMAC及新规则的配套安排或更明确口径，及时调整合规策略。
跟踪单一专利地域效力缺口案例，规划传统验证与单一专利的组合布局以规避保护失效。
topics.summary_en: In the first half of 2026, the Unified Patent Court published its second annual review and formally launched the Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre. This development strengthens alternative dispute resolution mechanisms for Unitary Patents and SEP/FRAND disputes. Judicial practice has become more rigorous, clarifying standards for equivalence infringement, single-product comparisons, and FRAND defenses. Courts emphasize strict procedural compliance regarding appeal deadlines, withdrawal rules, and evidence submission. Companies must remain vigilant against risks associated with functional claim limitations and optimize litigation strategies. Leveraging transparent case management systems allows for effective control of European patent enforcement costs and portfolio management.

Conduct rigorous audits of patent claims for products exported to Europe. Avoid using functional limitation language that may inadvertently trigger direct infringement liability fo...

Follow cases involving territorial validity gaps in Unitary Patents. Plan combined layouts of traditional national validations and Unitary Patents to avoid protection failures due...

topics.headline_zh: 欧洲SEP：UPC禁令与英国费率裁决
topics.headline_en: UPC Injunctions, UK FRAND Rates, and New EU Licensing Rules
topics.summary_zh: 2026年欧洲SEP格局剧变。UPC强化禁令执行力并签发反禁诉令，遏制中国法院临时许可；英国法院确立高额全球FRAND费率先例，且倾向仲裁解决争议。同时，修订版TTBER生效规范许可行为，NPE利用新平台加剧诉讼风险。车企与消费电子企业面临严峻合规挑战，需警惕多国司法对峙带来的商业中断风险。
key_points: UPC强化执法：曼海姆等分院积极签发禁令及反禁诉令(AASI)，明确拒绝中国法院‘临时许可’对欧洲程序的干扰，提升侵权成本。
英国费率标杆：英高院在三星诉中兴案中裁定3.92亿美元全球交叉许可费，确立可比协议法标准，并倾向于通过仲裁而非司法确定FRAND条款。
监管与反垄断：欧盟修订版TTBER生效，细化专利池与集体谈判指南；美欧在汽车专利联盟反垄断立场上出现显著分歧，增加跨国合规复杂性。
actions: 建立快速响应机制：收到UPC侵权通知须立即书面表达许可意愿，避免因程序瑕疵丧失FRAND抗辩权或面临即时禁令。
优化许可策略：评估加入Avanci/Sisvel等专利池的成本效益，避免陷入多国平行诉讼；审查过往低费率协议，防范被认定为sub-FRAND的风险。
加强内部合规：依据新版TTBER审查许可合同条款，特别是数据许可与交叉许可要求，确保符合欧盟竞争法规定。
watchlist: UKIPO SEP改革进程：关注英国即将完成的政策制定，特别是关于许可透明度及必要性审查的新规。
特斯拉5G案进展：该案可能成为继Unwired Planet后最重要的全球FRAND管辖权判例，影响后续诉讼策略。
中美司法冲突升级：密切监控UPC反禁诉令与中国反制措施的互动，评估对出口企业供应链稳定性的潜在冲击。

topics.china_impact_summary_zh: 这条长窗显示，SEP 风险已经从传统通信终端延伸到车载与 IoT，并在德国、英国和 UPC 三条诉讼线同步抬升。对中国企业来说，下一阶段重点不只是单案应对，而是把许可准备、诉讼预案和规则跟踪并行推进。
china_action_points: 涉及车载、蜂窝、Wi-Fi 或 IoT 出海业务的中国企业，应尽快核查专利池与双边许可覆盖，避免产品上市后再被动补谈。
在德国、英国和 UPC 有销售、供应链或客户落点的企业，应同步准备 FRAND 谈判材料、可比许可证据与禁令应对预案。
法务、标准与商务团队需要持续跟踪英欧 SEP 政策和竞争法变化，避免沿用单一市场的许可策略应对欧洲。
针对已进入欧洲市场的中国终端、部件与整车企业，建议把 SEP 许可预算、争议升级路径和管辖博弈纳入年度经营安排。

china_company_map.conclusion_zh: ZTE 当前以许可风险为主，且近期明显升温，建议优先前置许可准备与争议预案。

topics.summary_en: The European standard-essential patent landscape is undergoing significant transformation in 2026. The Unified Patent Court is strengthening enforcement by issuing anti-anti-suit injunctions to protect proceedings from foreign interim licenses, thereby increasing infringement costs. Concurrently, the UK High Court has established high-value global FRAND precedents through cross-licensing determinations, while appellate courts increasingly validate arbitration as a compliant mechanism for resolving licensing disputes. Regulatory frameworks are also evolving with the revised Technology Transfer Block Exemption Regulation providing new guidelines for patent pools and collective negotiations. These developments, combined with divergent transatlantic antitrust approaches, create complex compliance challenges for automotive and consumer electronics sectors. Companies must navigate heightened l...
key_points_en: UPC Enforcement: The court actively issues anti-anti-suit injunctions to prevent foreign interim licenses from disrupting European infringement proceedings, raising the stakes for...
UK Rate Determination: Courts are setting high global FRAND benchmarks via comparable license analysis and increasingly endorsing arbitration-linked offers as valid RAND proposals.
Regulatory Divergence: Revised EU competition rules clarify licensing behaviors, while US antitrust scrutiny of automotive licensing groups creates conflicting compliance obligatio...
actions_en: Establish rapid response protocols to immediately express licensing willingness upon receiving infringement notices, preserving FRAND defenses against immediate injunctions.
Evaluate participation in major patent pools like Avanci or Sisvel to mitigate parallel litigation risks, while reviewing historical agreements to avoid sub-FRAND classification.
Audit licensing contracts against the revised Technology Transfer Block Exemption Regulation, ensuring data and cross-license clauses comply with current EU competition law standar...
watchlist_en: UKIPO Policy Development: Monitor final regulatory reforms regarding licensing transparency and essentiality reviews expected in late 2026.
Global Jurisdiction Precedents: Track high-profile cases such as Tesla 5G that may define future global FRAND jurisdiction and litigation strategies.
Judicial Conflict Dynamics: Assess interactions between UPC anti-anti-suit measures and foreign countermeasures to evaluate potential impacts on export supply chains.

topics.headline_zh: 欧盟商标司法趋严与融资新政
topics.headline_en: Stricter EU Trademark Review, AI Squatting Risks, and New IP Financing Opportunities
topics.summary_zh: 2026年上半年，欧盟商标领域呈现司法审查趋严与政策红利释放并行的态势。法院强化对在先权利稳定性及恶意注册的审查，同时EUIPO推出中小企业基金降低注册成本，并发布报告推动IP融资创新。企业需警惕动态商标功能性及异议期权利失效风险，善用补贴与金融工具优化全球布局。
key_points: 司法审查趋严：CJEU裁定异议中在先权利须全程有效，普通法院撤销EUIPO决定强调全局评估声誉，动态商标若具纯技术功能将被驳回。
恶意注册风险：德国警示利用AI策略性抢注激增，无真实使用意图的注册面临无效风险；UKIPO警示假冒耗材危害，强调供应链合规。
政策与融资利好：EUIPO 2026中小企业基金提供费用报销，新报告建议释放5800亿欧元IP融资潜力，利摩日瓷器获首项手工艺GI保护。
actions: 建立权利动态监控机制，确保异议期间在先商标持续有效且合规使用，规避因权利灭失导致的维权失败。
尽早完成核心市场商标注册以防范AI驱动的恶意抢注，遭遇指控时积极收集在先使用证据提起无效宣告。
申请EUIPO中小企业基金降低欧盟商标申请成本，同时规范IP管理以探索知识产权质押融资等新渠道。
watchlist: 欧洲层面是否就“Obelix”案等裁判口径出台更明确的声誉评估配套指南。
欧盟IP融资政策落地进展及中小企业利用IP资产抵押融资的具体实操案例。
塞浦路斯等成员国在数字时代下对商标战略资产属性的规则细化及配套安排。
topics.summary_en: During the first half of 2026, the European trademark landscape is defined by heightened judicial scrutiny and emerging policy incentives. Courts are enforcing stricter standards for prior rights validity and rejecting registrations lacking genuine use intent, particularly those driven by AI tools. Concurrently, the EUIPO has launched initiatives to support SMEs through fee reimbursements and new frameworks for intellectual property-backed financing. Rights holders must navigate these developments by ensuring continuous compliance with use requirements and leveraging available financial instruments. The convergence of rigorous legal standards and supportive economic policies requires a proactive approach to portfolio management, emphasizing both defensive protection against bad-faith filings and strategic utilization of subsidies and collateralization options to optimize global brand pos...
key_points_en: Judicial Precedents: The CJEU mandates that earlier rights must remain valid throughout opposition proceedings, while the General Court emphasizes comprehensive reputation assessme...
Bad Faith & Compliance: Authorities warn of increased AI-driven speculative filings targeting unregistered signs in Germany, alongside UKIPO alerts on counterfeit supply chain risk...
Policy & Financing: The EUIPO SME Fund offers partial fee reimbursement for eligible enterprises, while new reports highlight pathways to unlock significant innovation financing th...
actions_en: Implement dynamic monitoring systems to ensure prior rights remain valid and compliant with use requirements during opposition periods, preventing loss of enforcement capabilities.
Secure early registrations in core markets to mitigate AI-driven squatting risks, and promptly gather evidence of prior use to challenge abusive applications or defend against infr...
Apply for EUIPO SME Fund reimbursements to reduce registration costs, while standardizing IP management practices to prepare for potential intellectual property-backed lending oppo...
watchlist_en: Potential issuance of clearer guidelines on reputation assessment methodologies following recent high-profile General Court rulings.
Implementation progress of EU IP financing policies and practical case studies involving SMEs utilizing intellectual property as loan collateral.
Regulatory refinements in member states regarding the treatment of trademarks as strategic digital assets and associated investment protections.

topics.headline_en: UKIPO Warns of Serious Risks from Counterfeit Toner and Ink Cartridges
topics.summary_zh: 英国知识产权局警示假冒碳粉危害，指出造假者常免责且不赔偿。全球假冒贸易额高达4670亿美元，多关联有组织犯罪。出口企业需建立合规溯源体系以防范供应链指控；进口商应加强供应商尽调，避免采购不明来源低价耗材，强化品牌保护与防伪措施。
key_points: 假冒碳粉致损严重且造假者常免责不赔
全球假冒贸易达4670亿美元并关联有组织犯罪
需建立合规溯源体系及加强供应商尽职调查
actions: 建立产品全链路合规溯源体系
强化进口供应商背景尽调与审核
升级防伪技术以应对假冒指控风险
watchlist: 欧盟及英国后续执法行动趋势
假冒耗材关联犯罪的司法判例更新
跨境供应链合规监管政策变化
topics.topic_name_zh: 假冒执法 / 品牌保护
topics.summary_en: The UK Intellectual Property Office has issued a warning regarding the significant harms caused by counterfeit ink and toner cartridges, particularly affecting small businesses. The blog highlights that while often perceived as low-risk, fake print consumables can cause severe operational damage. Global trade in counterfeit goods is estimated at USD 467 billion, frequently linked to serious organized criminal networks. Manufacturers of these fakes typically bear no liability or compensation responsibility for damages incurred. Consequently, businesses must implement strict procurement systems and compare product features to identify fakes on online platforms. Establishing robust IP compliance and traceability systems is essential to prevent involvement in counterfeit supply chains. Furthermore, conducting thorough supplier due diligence helps avoid procuring unverified, low-cost consumab...
key_points_en: Counterfeit print consumables cause serious business damage, yet manufacturers bear no liability or compensation responsibility.
Global counterfeit trade reaches approximately USD 467 billion, frequently linked to serious organized criminal networks.
Businesses must implement strict procurement systems and compare product features to identify fakes on online platforms.
actions_en: Establish strict IP compliance and traceability systems to prevent involvement in counterfeit supply chains.
Conduct thorough supplier due diligence to avoid procuring unverified, low-cost consumables.
Upgrade anti-counterfeiting technologies and verification protocols to mitigate counterfeit allegations.
watchlist_en: Subsequent enforcement trends by EU and UK authorities.
Updates on judicial precedents linking counterfeit consumables to organized crime.
Changes in cross-border supply chain compliance regulatory policies.
